title: Provident Fund (PF) Registration Service in Kerala- BookMyTM - Trademark & ISO Services in KeralaProvident Fund (PF) Registration

blocks.text: A provident fund is a mandatory retirement saving scheme introduced and managed by Government of India for benefiting the employees on retirement. An employee gives a portion of his/her salary to provident fund and the employer should make a contribution from their side. The money in the fund is kept and handled by Government and a retiree or their surviving family (nominee in case of diseased employee) withdraws the fund. In certain cases, provident fund pays out to the disabled employee, who is not in a condition to work further. Provident Fund (PF) registration is mandatory for all companies that have more than 20 employees.
Fill the details below for a callback.
Besides the contribution of the employee to EPF, the employer has to add equal amount which is inclusive of Employee Pension Scheme (EPS), which saves you from huge pension expenses.
In certain instances such as retirement, illness or demise of an employee, provident fund helps the employee/dependents by covering the financial risks they face in such situations.

The PF account of an employee can be transferred while he/she switching jobs. UAN (Universal Account Number) linked to the Aadhaar will help to facilitate the linking of previous accounts. The EPF account can be carry forward to the new employer rather than being closed down, which ensures the rate of return compounded over years.
An Employee can make claim over the EPF amount in case of any emergencies like illness, wedding or educational expenses, which will be great support for the employee.
Documents Required for Provident Fund Registration
PAN card of establishment/Proprietor’s PAN in case of Proprietorship firm.
Address proof that is in the name of the establishment. It can be rent agreement, Water, Electricity, Telephone bill
Specimen signature of directors and authorized signatories
Digital signature of the authorized applicant
In case of voluntary registration, consent of the majority of employees
Apply for Provident Fund Registration in 3 Easy Steps

items: Select any package suits your requirement
Fill up the form which will take less than 10 minutes
Provide details & upload documents required for Provident Fund Registration
Make payment through secure payment gateway.
Your queries will be answered quickly and effectively

PF Registration Certificate & UAN (universal Account Number)

items.q: What is the rate of PF contribution?
items.a: Both the employee and employer contribute 12% of the salary. The employers part consists of 12% of basic wages + dearness allowance + retaining allowance. If the number of employees is less than 20 in the firm, then the PF rate is 10%.

items.q: Will PF registration help in collection of pensions?
items.a: Yes. PF has a direct impact on the pension of an employee. The amount contributed by the employer towards EPF, 8.33% of it goes to the EPS, i.e., Employee Pension Scheme.

items.q: Shall an employee make claim of his EPF in emergencies?
items.a: Yes. An employee can make claim over his EPF in certain emergency situations such as illness, educational expenses or wedding expenses or him/herself or dependents.

items.q: What is the contribution for EPF both by employee and employer?
items.a: 12% is the basic salary of an employee is the contribution from employee side and the same amount should be contributed by employer too.
